title: Groups, choices and autocomplete

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Groups, choices and autocomplete; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Groups, choices and autocomplete
sources.claim: fieldset and legend name a related group of controls, especially radio choices sharing one question.

canonicalExplanation.summary: fieldset and legend name a related group of controls, especially radio choices sharing one question.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A delivery-method fieldset uses legend Delivery method and labels each radio option. Your goal is to group related choices and configure input purpose without relying on placeholder labels. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Paragraph and inline meaning; Relative and absolute URLs; Labels and input controls. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: fieldset and legend name a related group of controls, especially radio choices sharing one question. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Visual borders are optional, nested groups need clarity, and a heading alone may not create the same form-control group relationship. This draft therefore rejects: Radio buttons with the same name never need a group label.

includes: Use fieldset, legend, radio/checkbox semantics and autocomplete tokens appropriately.
